help.title: How to write your word list
help.intro: You don't have to follow any strict format — but a few simple habits below help us get every word right on the first try.
help.rule1Desc: Write each word or phrase on its own line.
help.rule2Title: Separate the word from its translation
help.rule2Desc: Put a dash, a colon, or a tab between the word and its translation.
help.rule3Desc: List the extra ones after a comma or a slash — we'll keep the first as the main translation and save the rest alongside it.
help.rule4Title: Want to add a quick clarification?
help.rule4Desc: Put it in parentheses right after the translation — it won't be mixed into the translation itself.
help.rule5Title: Not sure a translation is right?
help.rule5Desc: End the line with a question mark and we'll mark that word for you to double-check later.
help.rule6Title: Prefer to just paste messy text?
help.rule6Desc: That works too — paste a sentence, a list from class, or your own notes in any shape. We'll pick out the words, translate them, work out whether each one is a single word, a phrase, or a fixed expression, and add an example sentence where it helps.

csvImport.title: Import from CSV
csvImport.dropHint: Drag & drop a CSV file here, or click to browse
csvImport.expectedColumns: Expected columns: term, translation, wordType, notes, exampleSentence
csvImport.fillAllFields: Please fill all fields and select a file.
